2604.28082.title: Characterizing the Consistency of the Emergent Misalignment Persona
2604.28082.paper_summary: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) on narrowly misaligned data generalizes to broadly misaligned behavior, a phenomenon termed emergent misalignment (EM). While prior work has found a correlation between harmful behavior and self-assessment in emergently misaligned models, it remains unclear how consistent this correspondence is across tasks and whether it varies across fine-tuning domains. We characterize the consistency of the EM persona by fine-tuning Qwen 2.5 32B Instruct on six narrowly misaligned domains (e.g., insecure code, risky financial advice, bad medical advice) and administering experiments including harmfulness evaluation, self-assessment, choosing between two descriptions of AI systems, output recognition, and score prediction. Our results reveal two distinct patterns: coherent-persona models, in which harmful behavior and self-reported misalignment are coupled, and inverted-persona models, which produce harmful outputs while identifying as aligned AI systems. These findings reveal a more fine-grained picture of the effects of emergent misalignment, calling into question the consistency of the EM persona.
2604.28082.paper_summary_zh: 微調大型語言模型（LLMs）在狹義錯位數據上會導致廣義錯位行為，這種現象稱為新興錯位（EM）。雖然先前的研究發現有害行為與新興錯位模型中的自我評估之間存在相關性，但目前尚不清楚這種對應在不同任務中是否一致，以及它是否在微調領域之間有所變化。我們通過在六個狹義錯位領域（例如，不安全的代碼、風險財務建議、不良醫療建議）上微調 Qwen 2.5 32B Instruct，來描述 EM 人格的一致性，並進行包括有害性評估、自我評估、在兩個 AI 系統描述之間選擇、輸出識別和分數預測的實驗。我們的結果揭示了兩種不同的模式：一致人格模型，其中有害行為和自我報告的錯位相互關聯，以及反向人格模型，這些模型在識別為對齊的 AI 系統時產生有害輸出。這些發現揭示了新興錯位影響的更細緻圖景，質疑了 EM 人格的一致性。
2604.28082.author: Anietta Weckauff et.al.
2604.28082.authors: Anietta Weckauff, Yuchen Zhang, Maksym Andriushchenko
